name: krazy

metadata.name: krazy
metadata.summary: My own small package of uitilities I use.

documentation:
# krazy-utils

I am a beginner in programming and have recently started programming in python. krazy-utils is a simple package containing following functions I use frequently. I just wrote some of them so that I dont forget how to write them ;p

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.create_connection
documentation: Create sqlalchemy connection for postgresql

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.get_schema_names
documentation: Takes SQLAlchemy engine and returns schema names as list

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.get_table_names
documentation: Takes SQLAlchemy engine and returns schema wise table names as dictionary

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.table_search
documentation:
Searches for given table name in tables on Postgressql Server
Pass sqlalchemy engine with connection on

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.get_table_schema
documentation: Returns list containing table schema as dataframe and useful columns as list

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.table_delete
documentation: Deletes given tabe on postgresql server

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.create_table
documentation: Creates table in Postgresql server based on dataframe supplied

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.dbase_col_checker_adder
documentation: Checks if all columns in df exists in database and adds it not

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.dbase_writer
documentation:
writes data to table. Accepts following arguments for append:
True = append to existing data
False = deletes all rows and then insert data into existing table
delete_table = delete table, recreate table and writes data

functions.name: krazy.postgres_utilities.build_sql_select
documentation: builds select sql string based on table name, schema and follow_through given

functions.name: krazy.gsheet_initialize.google_workbook_open
documentation:
takes csjson as path to client secret json file
takes file_name as google sheet name
returns google workbook object
grspread documentation
# https://docs.gspread.org/en/latest/
gspread can work with service account login as well as OAuth client ID

functions.name: krazy.reconciler.df_import_clean_files
documentation:
import multiple files from a folder into pandas dataframe
dir:    Required:   Path to the folder containing the files
clean:  Optional:   Clean the dataframe. Default is True
df:     Optional:   Pass a dataframe. This is required if dir is false and clean is True
dtypes  Optional:   Pass a dictionary of data types for the columns

functions.name: krazy.reconciler.matcher
documentation: takes two dataframes and returns the possible columns on which dataframes reconciles

functions.name: krazy.reconciler.llm_extract_name
documentation:
Extract names from a text string and returns name as string
Returns: string

functions.name: krazy.reconciler.match_one_to_many
documentation:
Takes:
df_one: DataFrame from where one row matches to many from other
df_many: DataFrame from where many rows match to one from other
col_from_one: Column from df_one to match, amount column should be the last item in the list
col_from_many: Column from df_many to match, amount column should be the last item in the list
match_return_col: Column in which to return reco status
tolerance: Tolerance for matching for amounts. Default is 1 to -1

Returns a tuple of two DataFrames with reconciliation done in match_return_col
return [df_one, df_many]

functions.name: krazy.reconciler.match_one_to_one
documentation:
Takes:
df_one: DataFrame from where one row matches to many from other, dataframe name to use in match return column
df_two: DataFrame from where one rows match to one from other, dataframe name to use in match return column
col_from_one: Column from df_one to match, amount column
col_from_two: Column from df_many to match, and to sum
match_return_col: Column in which to return reco status
tolerance: Tolerance for matching for amounts. Default is 1 to -1

Returns a tuple of two DataFrames with reconciliation done in match_return_col

functions.name: krazy.reconciler.match_many_to_many
documentation:
Takes:
df_one: DataFrame from where one row matches to many from other, dataframe name to use in match return column
df_two: DataFrame from where one rows match to one from other, dataframe name to use in match return column
col_from_one: Column from df_one to match, amount column
col_from_two: Column from df_many to match, and to sum
match_return_col: Column in which to return reco status
tolerance: Tolerance for matching for amounts. Default is 1 to -1

Returns a tuple of two DataFrames with reconciliation done in match_return_col

writes data to table. Accepts following arguments for append:

functions.name: krazy.data_science.models_4std
documentation:
@params:
df          - required  - dataframe
features    - required  - list of features
dep_var     - required  - dependent variable as list
selection   - optional  - list of models to be applied
              accepted options for selection: ['gradientboostingregressor','logistic', 'svm', 'tree', 'knn']
test_size   - optional  - default is 0.2
random_state- optional  - default is 1
cv          - optional  - default is 2

Returns results from all models as a list of lists.
def models_auto_ml_100(df, features:list, y_col:str, test_size=0.3, random_state=1)->list:
@params:
df          - required  - dataframe
features    - required  - list of features
y_col       - required  - dependent variable as string
test_size   - optional  - default is 0.3
random_state- optional  - default is 1
Returns list of [model, [X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test], accuracy score]
y = df[y_col].copy()
X = df[features]
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=test_size, random_state=random_state)
model = AutoSklearnClassifier(
time_left_for_this_task=6*60,
per_run_time_limit=40
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_hat = model.predict(X_test)
acc = accuracy_score(y_test, y_hat)
return [model, [X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test], acc]

modules.name: krazy.utility_functions
documentation: List of useful pandas dataframe commands

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.files_identifier
documentation:
@params:
folder_path  -   directory path  :   from where files are to be imported
srch   -   optional       :   regex pattern to match with filename
excl   -   optional        :   regext pattern to exclude from filename

example of creating dataframe using dictionery

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.payment_ocr
documentation:
ocr on google pay and paytm screenshots
returns following:
1. if successfule: [success, template, paid_to, paid_on, amount, upi_id]
2. if image file not found: [FNF]
3. if template not found: [TNF]

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.number_converter
documentation: Converts number to int

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.progress_bar
documentation:
Call in a loop to create terminal progress bar
    @params:
        iteration   - Required  : current iteration (Int)
        total       - Required  : total iterations (Int)
        prefix      - Optional  : prefix string (Str)
        suffix      - Optional  : suffix string (Str)
        decimals    - Optional  : positive number of decimals in percent complete (Int)
        length      - Optional  : character length of bar (Int)
        fill        - Optional  : bar fill character (Str)
        printEnd    - Optional  : end character (e.g. "", "
") (Str)

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.forex_get_latest
documentation:
Gets latest forex rates from api.apilayer.com i.e. fixer api
@params
api_key    -   required    :   api key
base_cur   -   optional    :   base currency. USD by default.
get_cur    -   optional    :   All currencies by default. Pass string like 'SGD,INR' if specific currencies are required.

Returns response in json format
Functions
fixer API to get forex rates

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.forex_get_timeseries
documentation:
Gets forex rates for a timespan from api.apilayer.com i.e. fixer api
@params
api_key    -   required    :   api key
start_date -   required    :   start date in YYYY-MM-DD format
end_date   -   required    :   end date in YYYY-MM-DD format
base_cur   -   optional    :   base currency. USD by default.
get_cur    -   optional    :   All currencies by default. Pass string like 'SGD,INR' if specific currencies are required.

Returns response in json

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.forex_get_symbols
krazy.utility_functions.settings_importer
documentation:
@params:
file_path   - required  - path to the file
sheet_index - required  - index of the sheet in the file
columns required in the sheet:
- import_cols
- import_dtype (python data types)
- rename
- convert_int (y/n)
- convert_float64 (y/n)
- convert_date (y/n)

returns following in a list:
- import_cols as list
- import_dtype as dict
- col_rename as dic
- date_cols as list
- amt_cols as list
- int_cols as list
# functions from calculation utility brought here

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.col_dtype_converter
documentation:
converts date, amount and int columns from string to these dtypes. Date column can handle dates with - or /
Removes -, NA, na

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.excel_writer
documentation:
Checks if file / sheet already exists. If yes, appends data to existing file / sheet else creates new
file / sheet.

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.df_col_search
documentation:
Searches given string in column header in lower case and returns list of columns where string is found.
Case insensitive.

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.dtype_to_df
documentation: Accepts a dataframe and returns df containing column dtypes

functions.name: krazy.utility_functions.ageing_creator
documentation:
Creates following parameters and eturns dataframe with ageing:
df = pandas dataframe
col = column name of the column in dataframe. This should contain data in date format.
bins = list of integers containing the bins
bins_labels = labels to be used for bins    

Returns binned series which can be directly added to the source dataframe

classes.name: krazy.utility_functions.date_converter
documentation:
following attributes are available:
.final_date = gives date converted into proper date format
.validity = True / False showing valid input
.type = '/' or '-' or '' showing date separator used in input
.manual_mapped_vals = values which were not auto mapped but mapped based on d/m/y sequence
.manual_mapped_keys = keys which were not auto mapped but mapped based on d/m/y sequence

methods.name: krazy.utility_functions.TimeKeeper.get_log
